rulings.Case Title: BRIAN J. HECKTMAN et al VS. BING LOUIE LOUIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Cdismiss The X-Complaints Of Tami Twarog And Elizabeth A. Wright
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 5, 2010, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTS BRIAN HECKTMAN, SANDRA DELRAHIM, XON BURRIS, JANET BURRIS, ELIZABETH BURRIS, JOY DIFRANZA, SARA MENUCK'S Motion To dismiss The Cross-Complaints Of Tami Twarog And Elizabeth A. Wright. Motion granted without leave to amend. Cross-complainants no longer have standing and no justiciable controversy exists because of the mutual quitclaim deeds that were executed. This motion disposes of the entire cross-complaint. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT N. COLEN VS. MICHAEL HYATT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 5, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT MICHAEL HYATT'S Motion To Set Aside Judgment. Motion denied, as improperly noticed. Motion also denied on the merits. No basis to vacate the judgment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK MIZRAHI VS. DANNY AZAR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 5, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANTS DANNY AZAR, EISA AZAR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANY L KRAMER VS. TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 5, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted, no triable issue of material fact raised. Plaintiff's evidence merely demonstrates general retention of control over safety conditions at the worksite, but such retention is insufficient to impose liability on defendant. (See Hooker v. Dept. of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198; Madden v. Summit View, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal App 4th 1267.) No evidence of affirmative conduct on defendant's behalf. No evidence of an actionable omission by defendant as described in Hooker. No evidence that defendant affirmatively contributed to any danger. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY J YEE VS. EDWARD YEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 5, 2010, LINE 10, DEFENDANT JENNY GRANADOS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WITH SPECIFICITY THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT AND WHETHER IT IS WRITTEN, ORAL, OR IS TO BE IMPLIED BY CONDUCT. PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD A FIDUCIARY DUTY OR CONFIDENTIAL RELATIONSHIP. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GAYLYN HOLLOWAY et al VS. SECURICORP et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 5, 2010, LINE 11, CROSS-DEFENDANT SECURICORP DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST EITHER ATTACH A COPY OF THE WRITTEN CONTRACT OR SPECIFICALLY PLEAD ITS TERMS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. NEVONNE CHARLES et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 5, 2010, LINE 15, PLAINTIFF CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER FRANCIS WAS A TENANT AT THE SUBJECT PREMISES. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER FRANCIS WAS A GUEST. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER FRANCIS WAS ENGAGED IN DRUG-RELATED CRIMINAL ACTIVITY ON OR NEAR THE PREMISES. (CHARLES DECLARATION.) =(302/CWW)
